title: Topological boundaries of unitary representations
abstract: We introduce and study a generalization of the notion of the Furstenberg boundary of a discrete group $Γ$ to the setting of a general unitary representation $π: Γ\to B(\mathcal H_π)$. This space, which we call the "Furstenberg-Hamana boundary" of the pair $(Γ, π)$, is a $Γ$-invariant subspace of $B(\mathcal H_π)$ that carries a canonical $C^*$-algebra structure. In many natural cases, including when $π$ is a quasi-regular representation, the Furstenberg-Hamana boundary of $π$ is commutative, but can be non-commutative in general. We study various properties of this boundary, and give some applications.
